title: cerastium decalvans subsp. leontopodium
intro: Cerastium decalvans subsp. leontopodium, also known as the alpine whitlowgrass or woolly whitlowgrass, is a plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its woolly leaves and stems. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, making it an effective treatment for various skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Cerastium decalvans subsp. leontopodium include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
